email_security.title: SPF (Sender Policy Framework)
email_security.description: A DNS-based email authentication protocol that specifies which mail servers are authorized to send email on behalf of a domain.

email_security.title: DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail)
email_security.description: An email authentication standard that uses public-key cryptography to verify that a message was sent and authorized by the owner of a domain.

email_security.title: DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication)
email_security.description: An email authentication protocol that builds on SPF and DKIM to give domain owners control over how unauthenticated messages are handled.

email_security.title: Email Header Analysis
email_security.description: The process of examining RFC 5322 email headers to trace a message's delivery path, verify authentication results, and identify anomalies.

email_security.title: Email Spoofing
email_security.description: The forgery of email header fields to make a message appear to originate from a sender other than its true source.

email_security.title: Homoglyph Domains
email_security.description: Domains that substitute visually identical or near-identical Unicode characters to impersonate legitimate domains.

email_security.title: ARC Authentication
email_security.description: Authenticated Received Chain preserves email authentication results across forwarding hops.

email_security.title: Link-Display Mismatch
email_security.description: A phishing technique where the visible anchor text shows a different URL than the actual href destination.

email_security.title: Business Email Compromise (BEC)
email_security.description: A social engineering attack where adversaries hijack or spoof corporate email accounts for financial fraud.

email_security.title: Thread Hijacking
email_security.description: An attack where an adversary compromises a mailbox and replies to existing threads with malicious content.

email_security.title: QR Phishing (Quishing)
email_security.description: A phishing technique that embeds malicious URLs inside QR codes to bypass traditional email filters.

email_security.title: Credential Harvesting
email_security.description: The theft of usernames and passwords through fake login pages and phishing sites.

threat_intelligence.title: IOC Enrichment
threat_intelligence.description: The process of augmenting raw indicators of compromise with contextual threat intelligence.

threat_intelligence.title: Indicators of Compromise (IOCs)
threat_intelligence.description: Observable artifacts that indicate a system or network may have been breached.

threat_intelligence.title: Threat Intelligence
threat_intelligence.description: Evidence-based knowledge about existing or emerging cyber threats.

threat_intelligence.title: Threat Actor Profiling
threat_intelligence.description: The process of identifying and documenting threat group tactics, techniques, and infrastructure.

threat_intelligence.title: WHOIS Lookup
threat_intelligence.description: A protocol for querying domain registration information including registrant details and dates.

threat_intelligence.title: IP Reputation
threat_intelligence.description: A score or classification assigned to an IP based on historical malicious activity patterns.

threat_intelligence.title: Domain Reputation
threat_intelligence.description: A score or classification assigned to a domain based on historical behavior and threat data.

threat_intelligence.title: Certificate Transparency
threat_intelligence.description: A public logging framework that records all SSL/TLS certificates issued by Certificate Authorities.

threat_intelligence.title: Passive DNS
threat_intelligence.description: A historical record of DNS resolutions for domain-to-IP mappings and reverse lookups.

threat_intelligence.title: Attack Surface Management
threat_intelligence.description: The continuous process of discovering, inventorying, and reducing an organization's externally exposed digital assets.

threat_intelligence.title: DNS Security
threat_intelligence.description: Practices and technologies for protecting DNS infrastructure from manipulation and hijacking.

threat_intelligence.title: SSL/TLS Certificates
threat_intelligence.description: Digital certificates that authenticate server identity and enable encrypted communications.

threat_intelligence.title: Open Ports
threat_intelligence.description: Network ports on a host that are accepting connections and potential attack vectors.

threat_intelligence.title: Vulnerability Scanning
threat_intelligence.description: The automated process of identifying security weaknesses in systems and applications.

threat_intelligence.title: Data Removal Services
threat_intelligence.description: Services to request removal of personal information from data broker sites and search engines.

threat_intelligence.title: Breach Monitoring
threat_intelligence.description: Check if your email or domain has been exposed in known data breaches.

threat_intelligence.title: Ransomware
threat_intelligence.description: Malware that encrypts files and demands payment for restoration. Includes double extortion tactics.

threat_intelligence.title: Insider Threat
threat_intelligence.description: A security risk originating from within the organization, including malicious and negligent insiders.

forensics.title: Phishing Analysis
forensics.description: The forensic examination of suspected phishing emails, URLs, and landing pages.

forensics.title: Digital Forensics
forensics.description: The scientific discipline of identifying, preserving, and presenting digital evidence.

forensics.title: Incident Response
forensics.description: The organized approach to detecting, containing, eradicating, and recovering from security incidents.

forensics.title: Malware Analysis
forensics.description: The process of examining malicious software behavior, capabilities, and indicators.

forensics.title: Log Analysis
forensics.description: The examination of system and network logs to identify suspicious activities and patterns.

forensics.title: Timeline Analysis
forensics.description: The process of reconstructing a chronological sequence of events from multiple sources.

forensics.title: Memory Forensics
forensics.description: Analysis of volatile memory dumps to uncover running processes and artifacts.

forensics.title: Disk Forensics
forensics.description: Extraction and analysis of evidence from storage media and file systems.

forensics.title: Network Forensics
forensics.description: Capture and analysis of network traffic to investigate security incidents.

forensics.title: Forensic Artifacts
forensics.description: Common system and application artifacts used in digital investigations.

detection_engineering.title: MITRE ATT&CK Framework
detection_engineering.description: A knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques based on real-world observations.

detection_engineering.title: Sigma Rules
detection_engineering.description: A vendor-agnostic open standard for writing detection rules in YAML format.

detection_engineering.title: YARA Rules
detection_engineering.description: A pattern-matching tool for identifying and classifying malware samples.

detection_engineering.title: Threat Hunting
detection_engineering.description: The proactive search for adversary activity that has evaded existing security controls.

detection_engineering.title: Alert Triage
detection_engineering.description: The process of evaluating and prioritizing security alerts to focus on true positives.

detection_engineering.title: SIEM (Security Information and Event Management)
detection_engineering.description: Platforms that aggregate and analyze security events from across the enterprise.

detection_engineering.title: SOAR (Security Orchestration, Automation, Response)
detection_engineering.description: Platforms that automate security operations and incident response workflows.

detection_engineering.title: Detection-as-Code
detection_engineering.description: Managing detection rules as version-controlled code with CI/CD pipelines.

detection_engineering.title: API Security
detection_engineering.description: Practices and controls for protecting APIs from abuse and unauthorized access.

detection_engineering.title: Zero Trust Architecture
detection_engineering.description: Security model that assumes no implicit trust and requires continuous verification.

detection_engineering.title: Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR)
detection_engineering.description: Security tools that monitor endpoint activities and provide response capabilities.

detection_engineering.title: Network Detection and Response (NDR)
detection_engineering.description: Security tools that analyze network traffic for threat detection and response.

attack_types.title: Spear Phishing
attack_types.description: A targeted phishing attack directed at specific individuals or organizations.

attack_types.title: Typosquatting
attack_types.description: Registering domain names that are deliberate misspellings of legitimate domains.

attack_types.title: OAuth Phishing
attack_types.description: An attack that tricks users into granting malicious OAuth applications permissions.

attack_types.title: Social Engineering
attack_types.description: Psychological manipulation techniques to deceive people into making security mistakes.

attack_types.title: Supply Chain Attack
attack_types.description: An attack that compromises a trusted third-party vendor to reach the target.

attack_types.title: Watering Hole Attack
attack_types.description: An attack strategy where a website frequently visited by targets is compromised.

attack_types.title: Brute Force Attack
attack_types.description: A method that systematically tries large numbers of passwords or keys.

attack_types.title: Pass-the-Hash Attack
attack_types.description: A technique that uses password hashes to authenticate without knowing the actual password.

attack_types.title: Kerberoasting
attack_types.description: An attack targeting service accounts to extract their TGS tickets for cracking.

attack_types.title: Lateral Movement
attack_types.description: Techniques used to progressively move through a network after initial access.

attack_types.title: Privilege Escalation
attack_types.description: The process of gaining elevated access from an initial compromised account.

attack_types.title: Persistence Mechanisms
attack_types.description: Techniques used by attackers to maintain access after system reboots.

attack_types.title: Data Exfiltration
attack_types.description: Unauthorized transfer of data from an organization's network.

attack_types.title: Command and Control (C2)
attack_types.description: The infrastructure used by attackers to communicate with compromised systems.

attack_types.title: Living-off-the-Land
attack_types.description: Using legitimate system tools for malicious purposes to avoid detection.

attack_types.title: Fileless Malware
attack_types.description: Malware that operates entirely in memory without writing files to disk.

attack_types.title: Polyglot Attacks
attack_types.description: Attacks that combine multiple attack vectors or file formats to evade detection.

attack_types.title: Drive-by Download
attack_types.description: Malware that downloads automatically when visiting a compromised website.

osint_tools.title: MITRE ATT&CK Navigator
osint_tools.description: Interactive mapping tool for exploring and visualizing ATT&CK matrices.

osint_tools.title: Wayback Machine
osint_tools.description: Archive of web pages that can be used to view historical versions of websites.

osint_tools.title: Threat Intelligence RSS Feeds
osint_tools.description: Aggregated RSS feeds from security researchers and organizations.

osint_tools.title: Domain Dossier
osint_tools.description: Tool for investigating domain registration, DNS records, and historical data.

osint_tools.title: TI Platforms
osint_tools.description: Comprehensive threat intelligence platforms for IOC lookup and enrichment.

osint_tools.title: Vulnerability Databases
osint_tools.description: Comprehensive databases of known vulnerabilities including CVE details.

osint_tools.title: Threat Actor Trackers
osint_tools.description: Trackers monitoring known threat actor activities and infrastructure.

osint_tools.title: Brand Protection Tools
osint_tools.description: Tools for monitoring and protecting against brand impersonation.
